# Talents & Abilities {#sec-chapter-talents-abilities}

```{=typst}
#label("sec-chapter-talents-abilities")
```

![Illo 16: Talents Art](../assets/images/page037-img025.png){width="60%"}

*Illustration 16 — Talents & abilities chapter art (Intellect class). Placeholder; final art TBD. Dimensions: 1024×1024.*

{{< pagebreak >}}

Listen up. Talents are what you *are*. Abilities are what you *do*. One's always on, the other you have to activate. Both cost Development Points. Both define your hero. Don't confuse them.

Talents are passive. They modify your numbers, bend the rules in your favor, or grant you something you didn't have before. You buy them once and they just *work*, no activation, no maneuver cost, no "is it my turn yet?" They're the reason the Protector takes one more hit than everyone else and the Blade's crits leave a smoking crater.

Abilities are active. Spells, combat techniques, signature class powers, anything you have to *decide* to use. You spend your Action or Maneuver, the ability fires, something happens. Abilities follow the same Novice ? Adept ? Master chain as everything else in this game. See @sec-chapter-magic-system for spell chains specifically.

{{< pagebreak >}}

## Talent Categories

Before you start spending DP, know what you're buying. Talents fall into four buckets. Each one answers a different question about your hero.

| Category | What It Governs | Example |
|----------|----------------|---------|
| **Defense** | Staying alive. HP, DR, saves, resistances. | Tough, Iron Will, Unbreakable |
| **Offense** | Hitting harder. Bonus damage, extra attacks, critical effects. | Weapon Focus, Death Blow, Spell Weaver |
| **Utility** | Breaking the rules. Extra actions, rerolls, resource recovery. | Lucky, Combat Reflexes, Arcane Reservoir |
| **Support** | Helping allies. Healing boosts, protection auras, buffs. | Shield Wall, Blessed Touch, Divine Intervention |

You don't need to balance across all four. A Blade stacks Offense and Utility. A Protector stacks Defense and Support. That's how it should be, lean into what your class does.

{{< pagebreak >}}

## DP Costs

| Tier | DP Cost | Description |
|------|---------|-------------|
| **Novice** | 1 DP | Basic competency |
| **Adept** | 2 DP | Advanced mastery (requires Novice) |
| **Master** | 4 DP | Legendary expertise (requires Adept) |

::: {.callout-note}
## Talent Stacking: What Does and Doesn't Combine

Bonuses from **different-named talents** always stack. Tough (+2 HP) plus Iron Will (+2 to fear/charm saves), no conflict, both apply.

Bonuses from the **same talent taken multiple times** do *not* stack unless the talent explicitly says so. You can't buy Tough three times for +6 HP. One and done.

Bonuses from a talent **plus a skill or item** stack freely. Skill Focus (+1 to Stealth) stacks with your Stealth skill bonus (+1/+2/+3) and with any magic item bonus. The game assumes you'll stack. Build accordingly.

The one exception: **Discipline Master** (Master talent). It makes one Discipline count as *two* for meeting prerequisites. This doesn't stack with itself, you can't take it twice for the same Discipline to make it count as three. But you *can* take it for different Disciplines. A high-level Arcanist might have Discipline Master (Fire) and Discipline Master (Energy), meeting the prerequisites for spells that would otherwise be out of reach.
:::

{{< pagebreak >}}

## General Talents

Available to all classes. These are the talents any hero can pick up, regardless of calling. They're bread-and-butter, not flashy, but reliable.

| Talent | Tier | Category | Effect |
|--------|------|----------|--------|
| **Alert** | Novice | Utility | +2 initiative. You react before others even register the threat. |
| **Arcane Sensitivity** | Novice | Magic | +1 to spell attack rolls for one Discipline of your choice. You sense the flow of magic before others see it coming. |
| **Field Medic** | Novice | Support | When you restore HP to an ally (spell, kit, or ability), restore 1 additional HP. You've learned to stanch wounds with whatever is at hand. |
| **Fleet-Footed** | Novice | Utility | +5 ft Speed. Whether from a lifetime in the wilds or too many close calls in narrow alleys, you move faster than your enemies expect. |
| **Lucky** | Novice | Utility | Reroll one natural 1 per session. Fate favors you, or you're just too stubborn to fail. |
| **Shield Bearer** | Novice | Defense | +1 DR while wielding a shield. This is training, not enchantment, it stacks with magic shield bonuses. |
| **Skill Focus** | Novice | Utility | +1 to one skill (stacks with tier bonus). Pick your specialty and own it. |
| **Tough** | Novice | Defense | +2 max HP. You can take a hit that would drop anyone else. |
| **Weapon Training** | Novice | Offense | +1 damage with one specific weapon (e.g., longsword, greataxe, shortbow). Narrower than Weapon Focus but available to any class. |
| **Brutal Critical** | Adept | Offense | On a Strong attack result, add +2 damage. Your strikes find gaps in armor that others miss. |
| **Bulwark** | Adept | Defense | *Requires Shield Bearer.* When you take the Defend action, adjacent allies gain +1 DR until the start of your next turn. You don't just protect yourself, you hold the line. |
| **Combat Reflexes** | Adept | Utility | One extra reaction per round. While others hesitate, you act. |
| **Discipline Collector** | Adept | Utility | Gain one additional General Discipline. Your training has been broader than most. |
| **Dungeon Delver** | Adept | Utility | +2 to all checks to detect traps, secret doors, and hidden mechanisms. You've learned that dungeons punish the unobservant. |
| **Inspiring Presence** | Adept | Support | Once per encounter, as a Maneuver, one ally within 30 ft can immediately reroll a failed saving throw. They take the new result. Your confidence is contagious. |
| **Iron Will** | Adept | Defense | +2 to all saves vs fear and charm. Your mind is a fortress. |
| **Spell Reservoir** | Adept | Magic | *Requires Arcane Sensitivity.* Once per session, regain the use of one Novice or Adept spell you've already cast. Magic flows through you more freely than through others. |
| **Discipline Master** | Master | Utility | One specific Discipline counts as 2 for prerequisites. You haven't just mastered a discipline, you've transcended its normal limits. |
| **Indomitable** | Master | Defense | Once per session, when you would drop to 0 HP, drop to 1 HP instead and gain temporary HP equal to your level. You refuse to fall. |
| **Legendary Resilience** | Master | Defense | Once per session, when you fail a saving throw, you may choose to succeed instead. Your will reshapes reality in the moment it matters most. |
| **Spell Storm** | Master | Magic | Once per session, when you cast a spell that targets a single creature, it instead targets all creatures of your choice within a 15-ft radius of the original target. You don't cast spells, you unleash them. |

### Worked Example: Building a Talent Chain

Kael is a dwarf Blade at Level 4. He's got 3 DP to spend and he wants to become harder to kill without sacrificing his damage output. Here's his thought process.

**Current build:** Brawn +1, Agility +2. Blades Fighting (Adept). Stealth (Adept). He's got **Tough** (Novice) already, 12 HP instead of the base 10.

**Option A, Defense stack:** Buy Tough ? Adept. That's not how Tough works, it doesn't have an Adept version. It's a one-tier talent. Cross that off.

**Option B, Offense:** Buy **Weapon Focus** (Adept, Blade class talent). +1 damage with blades. Cost: 2 DP. Remaining: 1 DP. He could grab **Alert** (Novice) for +2 initiative, going first means one less attack coming his way.

**Option C, Utility:** Buy **Combat Reflexes** (Adept) for 2 DP. A second reaction per round means two opportunity attacks or two Shield Blocks. Then **Lucky** (Novice) for 1 DP, reroll one natural 1 per session.

He goes with Option B. The +1 damage from Weapon Focus applies every single attack, forever. Combined with Alert, he's hitting harder *and* sooner. At the table, that +1 from Weapon Focus turns a Weak (2 damage) into what would've been a borderline Standard (3 damage), and over a whole session, those extra points add up.

{{< pagebreak >}}

## Class Talents

Each class has 2-3 exclusive talents. These define your class identity mechanically, you can't get them any other way.

**Protector:** **Shield Wall** (Adept), adjacent allies gain +1 DR. **Unbreakable** (Master), once per session, ignore all damage from one attack.

**Blade:** **Weapon Focus** (Adept), +1 damage with chosen weapon category. **Death Blow** (Master), on Critical, deal maximum Strong damage + roll critical bonus twice.

**Arcanist:** **Spell Weaver** (Adept), maintain two concentration spells. **Arcane Reservoir** (Master), once per session, cast a Master spell twice.

**Shepherd:** **Blessed Touch** (Adept), healing spells always use Standard effect minimum. **Divine Intervention** (Master), once per session, prevent an ally's death.

### Worked Example: When Class Talents Combine

Lyra is a halfling Odd, Level 7. She took **Spell Weaver** (Adept) at Level 5, she can now maintain two concentration spells at once. At Level 7, she buys **Arcane Reservoir** (Master) for 4 DP.

Here's how a tough fight plays out:

**Round 1:** Lyra casts *Wall of Wind* (Adept, concentration), a barrier of howling air that deflects arrows and slows enemies. Then she casts *Haste* (Adept, concentration) on Kael. Two concentration spells, both running, because Spell Weaver says so. Kael now has an extra Action each turn and ranged attacks against the party are at -2.

**Round 3:** The DA drops a Young Dragon on the party. It breathes lightning. The party is scattered, wounded, and the Wall of Wind won't stop a dragon's breath.

Lyra activates **Arcane Reservoir.** She casts *Chain Lightning* (Master), her most devastating spell. It fires. Strong result: 12 damage to the dragon, 6 to every creature within 15 feet of it. Then Arcane Reservoir kicks in: she casts it *again*. Second roll: Standard, 8 more damage. The dragon has taken 20 damage in one turn from one caster.

That's the power of talent chains. Spell Weaver keeps her battlefield control running. Arcane Reservoir doubles her biggest gun. She spent 6 DP across two levels to build this combo. Every point paid off in that moment.

{{< pagebreak >}}

## Ability Chains

Abilities (including spells) follow the same Novice ? Adept ? Master chain structure. See @sec-chapter-magic-system for spell chains. The key distinction: **talents are always on; abilities must be activated.** A Protector with Shield Wall doesn't "activate" it, allies within reach just get +1 DR. A Blade using Precise Stab must spend their Maneuver and declare the ability. Know which is which before the dice hit the table.

::: {.callout-note}
## The Trap of "Buy Everything"

New players see the talent list and want it all. Here's the hard truth: a hero with twelve Novice talents spread across four categories is worse than a hero with three focused Adept talents that reinforce each other.

Pick a direction. If you're building a damage-dealer, stack Offense. Weapon Focus + Death Blow + Lucky (to fish for crits). If you're the party's shield, stack Defense. Tough + Iron Will + Unbreakable. The DP economy rewards focus. A scattered build is a weak build.

That said, one Utility talent is almost always worth it. **Lucky** (1 DP) has saved more heroes than any other single purchase. **Combat Reflexes** (2 DP) doubles your reactions. **Skill Focus** (1 DP) can turn a dabbler into a specialist. Grab one, then get back to your core build.
:::
